ad25c065eece.title: Get your menu online — 77% of diners check before visiting
ad25c065eece.description: No online menu found. Most diners check the menu before committing. Without one, you lose the customer at the decision point.
ad25c065eece.management_implication: Every day without a menu is a percentage of potential customers lost.

4dd7dadf9985.title: Identify and fix the recurring guest complaint
4dd7dadf9985.description: At 3.9/5 on Google, something specific is landing badly. Read the 5 most recent negative reviews, identify the common thread, and fix it at source. The rating won't move until the root cause does.
4dd7dadf9985.management_implication: Below 4.0, Google actively suppresses your venue in search results.

3ae0c4d386e5.title: Establish basic web presence — you don't exist online beyond Google
3ae0c4d386e5.description: No website, Facebook, or Instagram detected. Even a basic page improves discoverability and gives customers a reason to trust you before visiting.

d1d37f726c5a.title: Fix the digital shopfront — you're losing walk-ins
d1d37f726c5a.description: You deliver a strong experience but your online presence doesn't make it easy to act on. Can a new customer confirm your hours, see your menu, and book — all within 60 seconds on their phone? If not, that's your fix list.
d1d37f726c5a.management_implication: Interest without conversion is vanity traffic.

7ee8e45187fb.title: Ranked #3 of 3 locally — peers are ahead
7ee8e45187fb.description: At percentile 0.0, customers in this area have higher-rated alternatives. This isn't a crisis but it means you're competing on convenience rather than reputation.

dd3061e332ce.title: Guest experience trailing — investigate what peers do differently
dd3061e332ce.description: You trail peers by 1.6 points on experience. This isn't a label problem — it means customers are getting a measurably better experience on this dimension from your competitors.
